─[ mkdocs-panzoom-plugin Assets Lost in Serve Mode ]─ ┐

name: mkdocs-panzoom-serve-assets description: mkdocs-panzoom-plugin assets vanish under mkdocs serve due to livereload temp dirs user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Bash, Read, Edit

mkdocs-panzoom-plugin Assets Lost in Serve Mode

The mkdocs-panzoom-plugin copies its CSS/JS assets to site_dir in the on_post_build hook. Under mkdocs serve, the dev server uses a temporary directory that gets recreated on every livereload cycle — the assets are copied once, then lost on the next rebuild.

Symptoms

  • Panzoom overlay/zoom doesn’t activate on images or mermaid diagrams
  • Browser console shows 404 for assets/stylesheets/panzoom.css, assets/javascripts/panzoom.min.js, assets/javascripts/zoompan.js
  • The <link> and <script> tags are present in the HTML (injected by on_config), but the files aren’t served

Fix

Stage the plugin’s assets into docs_dir before starting mkdocs serve. Files in docs_dir are copied into every build, including livereload rebuilds.

The assets live in the installed package:

site-packages/mkdocs_panzoom_plugin/custom/panzoom.css
site-packages/mkdocs_panzoom_plugin/custom/zoompan.js
site-packages/mkdocs_panzoom_plugin/panzoom/panzoom.min.js

Copy them to:

<docs_dir>/assets/stylesheets/panzoom.css
<docs_dir>/assets/javascripts/zoompan.js
<docs_dir>/assets/javascripts/panzoom.min.js

Snags

  • site_url subpath breaks local assets too. If mkdocs.yml has site_url: https://example.com/subpath/, mkdocs generates absolute paths like /subpath/assets/... on sub-pages. Locally the server runs at /, so all assets 404. Override site_url to http://localhost:<port>/ for dev serving.
  • Affects all custom assets, not just panzoom. extra_css and extra_javascript entries suffer the same site_url subpath issue.
  • on_post_build vs on_serve: The plugin could fix this upstream by using on_serve or by placing assets in docs_dir itself. As of v0.5.2, it doesn’t.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
